entries.rationale: 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6) personal-privacy exemption marker indicates redaction has already occurred; suppression context (1.md:63, 1.md:427).

Legal-entity name suffixes per FRCP Rule 17 indicate corporate party not natural-person PII (1.md:232, 1.md:407, 1.md:409).
Lab specimen accession identifier per NUCC Box 23 CLIA context (1.md:121, 1.md:452).
UBL cac:PayeeFinancialAccount/cbc:ID; checking account numbers commonly 9-12 digits, high SSN-shape confusable (1.md:91, 1.md:442).
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 26 Patient's Account No.; institutional account ID. Distinct from the (ssn, financial) override on the same keyword (1.md:116, 1.md:451).
UBL BuyerReference / account-reference variant; sequential identifier (1.md:153, 1.md:465).
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 18 admission date; HIPAA Safe Harbor (C) treats admission date as PHI but the label suppresses DOB-confusion only on medical doctype (1.md:131, 1.md:399).
UB-04 admission identifier per CMS Medicare Claims Processing Manual Pub. 100-04 Ch. 25 (1.md:119, 1.md:453).
Amount-due labels on billing statements mark payment totals; adjacent numbers are account identifiers, not SSNs.
Balance-due labels on billing statements mark outstanding totals; adjacent numbers are account identifiers, not SSNs.
Numeric label barcodes on specimens; (npi) scope rejected as barcode contents pattern-defined by GS1/HIBC, not directly NPI confusable. Lowered from 0.45 per 1.md:137, 1.md:234.
Drug batch ID; near 10-digit string usually batch not NPI per 21 CFR Part 207. SSN fanout dropped (1.md:139, 1.md:254).
Bed identifiers are short numeric tokens; weak DOB-shape confusable on census sheets. Rescoped from (ssn,) per 1.md:136, 1.md:233.
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 33 billing-provider address; provider not patient address (1.md:269, 1.md:386).
CPT/HCPCS 5-digit not DEA-shaped; weak relevance. (ssn,npi) scope rejected as catastrophic FN risk per 1.md:128-129, 1.md:235.
Travel/reservation identifier; variable-length sequential (1.md:151, 1.md:466).
Bank branch name in transaction blocks; not customer PII (1.md:249, 1.md:412).
Bank branch address on receipts and statements; not customer PII (1.md:278, 1.md:380).
Document-type label, not patient name (1.md:242, 1.md:416).
Court case-number format per 28 U.S.C. § 1914 and FRCP Rule 10; canonical 9-digit-shape confusable for SSN/NPI/DEA on federal court dockets (1.md:87, 1.md:437).
Court case-number format per 28 U.S.C. § 1914 and FRCP Rule 10; canonical 9-digit-shape confusable for SSN/NPI/DEA on federal court dockets (1.md:87, 1.md:437).
Court case-number format per 28 U.S.C. § 1914 and FRCP Rule 10; canonical 9-digit-shape confusable for SSN/NPI/DEA on federal court dockets (1.md:87, 1.md:437).
Variant of MRN per HIPAA Safe Harbor 45 CFR 164.514(b)(2)(i)(H) (1.md:115, 1.md:454).
Check-number labels in bank statements hold sequential payment identifiers.
Claim-number labels in insurance statements hold case-tracking IDs, not SSNs.
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 11b Other Claim ID; CMS ICN/DCN at UB-04 FL 37 (1.md:120, 1.md:455).
c/o Clerk of Court address is a court office address, not a party-identifier address (1.md:262, 1.md:382).
Confirmation-number labels in transaction records hold transaction-tracking codes.
Booking/order confirmation; variable-length sequential identifier (1.md:148, 1.md:467).
Control-number labels on W-2 (box d) hold the employer-assigned payroll ID, not the recipient SSN.
Federal courthouse addresses are public locations, not party PII (1.md:263, 1.md:383).

HHS OCR breach notices reference covered entity; 10-digit string in this context is typically entity-NPI cross-reference, but suppression weight reflects potential real NPI presence (1.md:198, 1.md:421).
CPT codes are 5-digit per NUCC Box 24D; weak 10-digit confusable but appears alongside NPI on CMS-1500 (1.md:190, 1.md:422).
Vendor-assigned customer identifier; variable-length. NPI fanout dropped per 1.md:152, 1.md:468.
CMS Document Control Number; synonym for ICN at UB-04 FL 37 (1.md:179, 1.md:446).
Defendant captions are name-adjacent; FRCP 5.2 requires SSN already redacted in caption blocks (1.md:43).
Department name in hospital letterhead; not patient name (1.md:241, 1.md:417).
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 18 discharge date; same scoping as admission date (1.md:132, 1.md:400).
DEA Diversion Control Division FOIA Library publishes redacted MOAs and audit reports; suppression context (1.md:222, 1.md:389).
Standalone 'docket' token is weaker than 'docket number'; SSN-shape only per US Courts glossary (1.md:50, 1.md:438).
Court docket number; canonical confusable for SSN/NPI/DEA on captions (1.md:88, 1.md:439).
Court docket number; canonical confusable for SSN/NPI/DEA on captions (1.md:88, 1.md:439).
Court docket number; canonical confusable for SSN/NPI/DEA on captions (1.md:88, 1.md:439).
UBL cbc:DueDate is invoice payment due date, not DOB (1.md:302, 1.md:403).
Effective-date labels mark when account terms or policies begin.
EIN format XX-XXXXXXX shares 9-digit shape with SSN; IRS Form W-9 Part I provides as alternative TIN (1.md:95, 1.md:440).
Employer's EIN label (W-2 box b) is adjacent to the SSN box (box a); the 9-digit EIN in that position is not an SSN.
Encounter identifier per HIPAA Safe Harbor (J) account-numbers category (1.md:118, 1.md:456).
Encounter identifier per HIPAA Safe Harbor (J) account-numbers category (1.md:118, 1.md:456).
Attorney signature blocks per FRCP and 28 U.S.C. § 1654; attorney names appear publicly in case captions (1.md:230, 1.md:408).
Legal-entity name suffixes per FRCP Rule 17 indicate corporate party not natural-person PII (1.md:232, 1.md:407, 1.md:409).
Ex-dividend-date labels appear in brokerage statements; very specific to equity distributions.
5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6) personal-privacy exemption; marking exists because PII has been redacted (1.md:63, 1.md:96).

5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(7)(C) law-enforcement personal privacy (1.md:64, 1.md:97).
Expiration-date labels appear on credit-card statements and benefits notices.
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 11; 9-character alphanumeric per 5 U.S.C. § 8101 federal-employee work-injury claims (1.md:180, 1.md:447).
Fiscal-year labels on financial statements mark the accounting period.

DEA Form 224 registration application released through DEA FOIA carries DEA registration #; the form name signals suppression context (1.md:221, 1.md:390).
Formulary IDs and codes adjacent to medication lists are non-DEA institutional codes (1.md:211, 1.md:391).

USPS Domestic Mail Manual sender block; letterhead From: addresses are typically business not natural-person PII (1.md:285, 1.md:384).
USPS Domestic Mail Manual From: block typically business not natural-person (1.md:256, 1.md:415).
Fund-name labels on investment statements hold mutual fund or ETF names.
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 11 Insured's Policy, Group, or FECA Number (1.md:175, 1.md:457).
HCPCS codes per NUCC Box 24D; same 5-digit shape as CPT (1.md:191, 1.md:423).
Hospital name in letterhead; institution not patient PII (1.md:239, 1.md:418).
CMS Internal Control Number; 13-digit Medicare claim ID at UB-04 FL 37 (1.md:178, 1.md:448).
Legal-entity name suffixes per FRCP Rule 17 indicate corporate party not natural-person PII (1.md:232, 1.md:407, 1.md:409).
Institution-name labels on financial forms identify the holding bank or brokerage.
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 1a; payer-assigned member ID, high SSN-shape confusable (1.md:172, 1.md:458).
21 CFR 1304.04 inventory line items are non-DEA codes; DEA registration # appears in the document header, not on the inventory line (1.md:212, 1.md:392).
21 CFR 1304.04 inventory identifier is institution-internal, not DEA registration #. Rescoped from (ssn,) per 1.md:138, 1.md:393.
UBL cbc:ID invoice identifier; canonical sequential-9-digit confusable for SSN (1.md:90, 1.md:443).
UBL cbc:ID invoice identifier; canonical sequential-9-digit confusable for SSN (1.md:90, 1.md:443).
Issue-date labels on financial docs mark document or instrument generation date; weight is lighter because 'issue date' is also found near personal records.
Generic document issue date; date-shaped string near issue date label is overwhelmingly issue not DOB (1.md:310, 1.md:406).
Issuer labels on bond/securities statements hold the issuing entity's name, not a personal name.
Exemption 7 threshold language per DOJ FOIA Guide chapter on Exemption 7 (1.md:69, 1.md:431).
Legal-entity name suffixes per FRCP Rule 17 indicate corporate party not natural-person PII (1.md:232, 1.md:407, 1.md:409).
Legal-entity name suffixes per FRCP Rule 17 indicate corporate party not natural-person PII (1.md:232, 1.md:407, 1.md:409).
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 14 last-menstrual-period date qualifier (1.md:294, 1.md:401).
Loan-number labels in mortgage and credit statements hold account identifiers, not SSNs.
Drug lot numbers per 21 CFR 201.18 are alphanumeric pharmaceutical batch IDs. NPI fanout dropped — lot codes do not 10-digit-shape match NPIs (1.md:133, 1.md:230).
Drug lot numbers per 21 CFR 201.18 are alphanumeric pharmaceutical batch IDs. NPI fanout dropped — lot codes do not 10-digit-shape match NPIs (1.md:133, 1.md:230).
Maturity-date labels appear in fixed-income and CD account statements.
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier; 11-char alphanumeric replacing SSN-based HICN since 2020-01-01 under MACRA 2015 SSNRI (1.md:177, 1.md:449).
Medical center name; institution not patient PII (1.md:240, 1.md:419).
Medical Record Number per HIPAA Safe Harbor 45 CFR 164.514(b)(2)(i)(H); per-institution 6-10 digit identifier with high SSN-shape confusable rate (1.md:114, 1.md:450).
NUCC v13 Item 1a/Item 6 reference; payer-assigned identifier (1.md:173, 1.md:459).
Member-number labels in credit-union and insurance statements hold membership IDs.
Merchant name on receipts; not customer PII (1.md:250, 1.md:413).
Merchant street address on receipts and 1099 PAYER address blocks; not customer PII (1.md:277, 1.md:381).
Product model identifier; weaker shape match per HIPAA Safe Harbor (M) device serials (1.md:159, 1.md:469).
Medical Record Number per HIPAA Safe Harbor 45 CFR 164.514(b)(2)(i)(H); per-institution 6-10 digit identifier with high SSN-shape confusable rate (1.md:114, 1.md:450).
Bank legal-name suffix per Federal Reserve MDRM (e.g., Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.); institution not customer name (1.md:248, 1.md:414).
NCPDP pharmacy ID is 7-digit, format-distinct from DEA 9-char alphanumeric. Suppresses DEA-shape false positives near pharmacy provider blocks (1.md:210, 1.md:394).
10/11-digit NDC near 'NDC' is overwhelmingly NDC not NPI per FDA NDC Directory (1.md:134, 1.md:424).
10/11-digit NDC near 'NDC' is overwhelmingly NDC not NPI per FDA NDC Directory (1.md:134, 1.md:424).
DOJ OIG audit reports cite provider NPIs in published findings; partial confusable context (1.md:199, 1.md:420).
E-commerce sequential ID per UBL OrderReference (1.md:147, 1.md:470).
Lab/imaging order number; institutional identifier. NPI fanout dropped per 1.md:123, 1.md:460.
Insurance company address in claim header; not patient PII (1.md:271, 1.md:387).
Exemption 6 and 7(C) statutory phrase per DOJ FOIA Guide (1.md:66, 1.md:432).
NCPDP pharmacy ID confusable with DEA when shape overlaps; per DEA Pharmacist's Manual. Rescoped from (npi,) per 1.md:125, 1.md:395.
Generic physician ID label may legitimately co-occur with DEA registration number; low suppression weight to preserve recall. Rescoped from (npi,) per 1.md:126, 1.md:396.
Plaintiff captions are name-adjacent; FRCP 5.2 requires SSN already redacted in caption blocks (1.md:44).
Plan-name labels on 401k/403b statements hold the retirement plan sponsor name.
UBL cac:OrderReference/cbc:ID; high SSN-shape confusable. NPI fanout dropped — UBL does not carry healthcare provider IDs (1.md:93, 1.md:441).
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 9a, Box 11 (1.md:176, 1.md:461).
Pharmacy fill ID near DEA-shaped string is overwhelmingly the pharmacy fill ID per 21 CFR 1306.05; not DEA registration. Rescoped from (ssn,npi) per 1.md:124, 1.md:397.
Generic provider ID label may co-occur with DEA; low weight preserves recall. Rescoped from (npi,) per 1.md:127, 1.md:398.
Sequential quote identifier (1.md:154, 1.md:471).
Variable-length point-of-sale identifier (1.md:149, 1.md:472).
Literal token 'redacted' indicates redaction metadata (1.md:65, 1.md:98, 1.md:433).

DOJ FOIA Reference Guide §IX redaction marker; on real FOIA responses sits next to redacted PII (1.md Pool B narrowing).
DOJ FOIA Reference Guide §IX redaction marker; on real FOIA responses sits next to redacted PII (1.md Pool B narrowing).
DOJ FOIA Reference Guide §IX redaction marker; on real FOIA responses sits next to redacted PII (1.md Pool B narrowing).
Variable-length sequential identifier per UBL BuyerReference; common 9-digit shape. NPI fanout dropped per 1.md:145, 1.md:473.
API/system request identifier; variable-length (1.md:157, 1.md:474).
Booking variant; variable-length sequential (1.md:160, 1.md:475).
DOJ FOIA Reference Guide §IV; document-context indicator with weak direct PII adjacency (1.md:68, 1.md:434).
DOJ FOIA Reference Guide §IV; document-context indicator with weak direct PII adjacency (1.md:68, 1.md:434).
Return address per USPS DMM is the sender block; commonly business address (1.md:284, 1.md:385).
Room numbers 3-4 digits; weak DOB-shape confusable (e.g. 'room 1234' looks like a DOB stem). Rescoped from (ssn,npi) per 1.md:135, 1.md:232.
Room numbers 3-4 digits; weak DOB-shape confusable (e.g. 'room 1234' looks like a DOB stem). Rescoped from (ssn,npi) per 1.md:135, 1.md:232.
ABA RTN is exactly 9 digits with check digit per Federal Reserve E-Payments Routing Directory; very high shape confusable (1.md:92, 1.md:444).
Pharmacy fill ID near DEA-shaped string is overwhelmingly the pharmacy fill ID per 21 CFR 1306.05; not DEA registration. Rescoped from (ssn,npi) per 1.md:124, 1.md:397.
State Bar Number following attorney name in caption block; non-PII identifier (1.md:231, 1.md:410).
Product serial number per HIPAA Safe Harbor (M) device serials category. NPI fanout dropped per 1.md:158, 1.md:476.

NUCC CMS-1500 Box 24A Date(s) of Service; date-shaped string near service-date label is overwhelmingly DOS not DOB. Scope strictly medical to avoid catastrophic FN on non-medical documents (1.md:130, 1.md:402).
NUCC CMS-1500 Box 32 service-facility address; not patient PII (1.md:270, 1.md:388).
Settlement-date labels appear in brokerage and banking activity records.
Lab specimen identifier; institutional non-PII per NUCC Box 23 CLIA context (1.md:122, 1.md:462).
Bank statement period dates per Regulation E; not DOB (1.md:301, 1.md:404).
Industry variant of NUCC CMS-1500 Box 1a Insured's I.D. Number (1.md:174, 1.md:463).
Customer-support case identifier (1.md:156, 1.md:477).

TIN field per IRS Form W-9 Part I and NUCC CMS-1500 Box 25; SSN/EIN alternative shares 9-digit shape (1.md:94, 1.md:445). De-weighted 0.55 -> 0.35 at the S3 D1 gate (2026-06-11, Jesse-reviewed): a TIN field is the designated label for a sole proprietor's SSN, so at 0.55 the suppression dropped label-boosted SSNs below the balanced cutoff; 0.35 is the design 02 section-2.3 ambiguous-term weight (boosted SSNs surface, bare 9-digit shapes still drop at balanced).
Tax-year labels on 1040/W-2/1099 forms are designed to mark the filing year, not birth year.
Support-system identifier; variable-length sequential (1.md:155, 1.md:478).
USPS IMpb 22-digit domestic / 13-char alphanumeric international per USPS Publication 199 (1.md:146, 1.md:479).
Transaction date label per 1099 instructions; date-shaped string overwhelmingly transaction not DOB (1.md:300, 1.md:405).
Variable-length transaction identifier; Regulation E electronic fund transfer context (1.md:150, 1.md:480).
Transit-number labels in banking hold ABA routing codes; 9-digit but not SSNs.
Legal-entity name suffixes per FRCP Rule 17 indicate corporate party not natural-person PII (1.md:232, 1.md:407, 1.md:409).
Ward/unit IDs on hospital routing slips; A5 audit had no FN evidence either way, KEEP at current scope.
Legacy Unique Physician Identification Number per NUCC Box 17a qualifier 1G; appears on archival pre-2007 documents (1.md:192, 1.md:425).
Case caption v. (versus) separates public party names (1.md:233, 1.md:411).
UB-04 visit identifier; HIPAA Safe Harbor 45 CFR 164.514(b)(2)(i)(J) account-numbers category (1.md:117, 1.md:464).
DOJ FOIA Reference Guide §IX redaction notation (1.md:67, 1.md:435).
